steps.title: Identify the antecedent and required form
steps.content: The blank precedes "methodology" and must be a possessive. The antecedent is "the technique" (the Meisner technique) — singular and referring to a thing, not a person.

steps.title: Apply possessive pronoun agreement
steps.content:
Rule: use the possessive pronoun "its" for singular non-person antecedents; never use contractions in place of possessives.
  • Choice A — "they're": contraction of "they are" — cannot function as a possessive, and plural "they" doesn't match singular "the technique". ✗
  • Choice B — "its": singular possessive pronoun — correctly modifies "methodology" and agrees with singular "the technique". ✓
  • Choice C — "it's": contraction of "it is" — reads as "it is methodology", which is grammatically wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — "their": plural possessive — does not agree with singular "the technique". ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
